project.name: BMP :: bmp-config-server (runtime configuration)
project.description:
Session 7. Spring Cloud Config Server — serves each service's hot-reloadable properties from config-repo/ in THIS SAME GitHub repo (not a separate one; simplest thing that works for a 3-person team). A GitHub webhook hits /monitor here on every push; spring-cloud-config-monitor figures out which services' config files changed and publishes a RefreshRemoteApplicationEvent on Spring Cloud Bus (Kafka transport, reusing the same broker bmp-notification already needs), which every service picks up automatically — no redeploy, no manual /actuator/busrefresh call needed once the webhook is wired.
NOT in scope here: secrets (DB passwords, JWT signing key, API keys). Those stay as environment variables set outside git, per DATABASE_SETUP.md's existing rule — this server is for things safe to see in a public-ish diff (feature flags, timeouts, log levels, OTP TTLs), not credentials.
